rule: Catch the specific exception you can handle;
why: B requires catching narrowest exception type and forbids bare except/except Exception without reraise

rule: Re-raise with cause preserved: raise ServiceError(...)
why: B requires raise...from to preserve or explicitly suppress chain, forbids accidental drop via bare raise

rule: Log at the boundary where the error
why: B requires logging exactly once at the point handled, forbids re-logging at multiple layers

rule: Include operation context in the log
why: B requires structured context with operation name and relevant IDs, not just the exception string
B forbids silent swallowing and requires logging of every caught-not-reraised exception

rule: Timeouts and retries live at the
why: B requires hard cap on retry attempts/elapsed time, forbidding unbounded retry loops
